concept: Rise / Ascend Without Effort / Come Up
description: To rise: smoke from the hearth, bread under its cloth, the sun over the ridge (though the sun has its own poetry), a person from sleep. Effortless or inward ascent — 'kalei' (climb) is its effortful cousin, 'lepa' (fall) its opposite, sharing almost no sounds with either.
sound_symbolism: Bright 'ri' lifts, 'he' is the breath that carries it — rising as exhale, the smoke's own physics.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive: 'whunei rihe.' (The breath rises.) 'lopia lue nuwera rihe.' (The child rises from bed.) Of bread: 'nuora rihe.' (The food rises.) Contrast 'kalei' (climb, effortful ascent) and 'lepa' (fall, its opposite).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Thoughts rise like bubbles — unbidden, unowned, observable. The verb of meditation's first discovery.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The day was told by risings: sun, smoke, bread, birds. To rise was to begin.
